notes: Smallest round-trip: one plain-text prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use. OpenCode runs the turn headlessly via `opencode run` (blocking); session state lives in the SQLite store, observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as a UUID-keyed ses_… session. Phases assert: (1) session born in ready, (2) PID bound, (3) the prompt drives ready→working, (4) the turn completes back to ready — the canonical ready→working→ready arc. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession (if the process scanner emits one) cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that turn1_done chains off. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — a no-tool turn never gates on a permission ask. TotalTokens>0 is a user-observable signal but has no metric form in the phase DSL, so it is asserted via the parser's PerTurnContribution, not a phase line.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
User prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
The single turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session
